title: Over-Compression + JPIS Stability Bridge
hypothesis: Over-compressed models (low core PR) gain from mild PR expansion, and that expansion gain predicts lower pressure fragility (pressure_auc from JPIS). The mediation path core_pr -> expansion_gain -> pressure_auc links representation geometry to dynamic stability.
value_to_investors: Core PR strongly predicts expansion resilience (r=0.905, p=0.002): models with higher intrinsic dimensionality are immune to dimensional perturbation. However, pressure fragility is orthogonal to this axis, meaning deployment risk assessment requires BOTH geometric and dynamic stability testing.
value_to_researchers: PR dimensionality and dynamic stability are genuinely orthogonal measurement axes. Core PR predicts expansion resilience but NOT noise fragility. This falsifies simple 'compression causes fragility' models and supports a multi-axis characterization framework for LLM behavior.
identification_strategy: Interventional: inject orthogonal subspace expansion at varying strengths into hidden states, measure accuracy change. Cross-reference with JPIS perturbation data (Gaussian noise injection). Coarse sweep: 6 layers x 4 strengths x 3 seeds per model.
falsification_tests: If expansion gain correlated with pressure_auc, Spearman p < 0.05 (FALSIFIED: p=0.61)
If core_pr predicted expansion gain, Spearman p < 0.05 (CONFIRMED: r=0.905, p=0.002)
If mediation path existed, indirect effect CI would exclude zero (FALSIFIED: CI=[-0.83, 0.62])
success_criteria: At least 6 models with both expansion and JPIS data
Mediation path test with indirect effect estimate

data_lineage.provenance: 8 models, NF4 quantization, 32 calibration prompts per model
data_lineage.transformations: Mean accuracy at mild expansion band (0.05-0.10) minus baseline
pressure_auc averaged across domains per model

out_of_sample_validation_plan: Leave-one-out cross-validation on core correlation. Additional models from registry can serve as holdout.
uncertainty_methods: Bootstrap 5000 for mediation indirect effect CI
robustness_checks: Multiple layer positions tested (6 coarse layers per model)
Both Spearman and Pearson correlations reported
Per-domain (math vs factual) correlations checked separately
business_implication: Deployment risk requires multi-axis testing: representation geometry (PR) predicts expansion resilience but NOT noise robustness. A model can be geometrically well-structured yet dynamically fragile, or vice versa.
scientific_implication: PR dimensionality and dynamic stability are orthogonal axes. Core PR strongly predicts expansion resilience (r=0.905, p=0.002) but does not predict noise fragility. This falsifies simple compression-causes-fragility models.
